enrichment.summary: Kyle records a vlog the morning after May 2nd, 2014, reflecting on his habit of cataloging things (books, running times) to build a personal relationship with them, his workflow for bulk tasks while smoking hashish, a bounced check from a split bank account, forgetting his keys and having his mom bring them, and observations about squirrels and his pet hamsters and geckos.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Creating organized maps of things — books, times, distances — builds a personal relationship with them and keeps them present in your world
The only way to accomplish large tasks is to sit and do them all day in one sustained push; incremental work is less effective
Squirrels, hamsters, and geckos are smarter than people give them credit for; their intelligence tracks with their eyesight
Refuses to pay the full $70 NSF fee; successfully negotiated it down to $35
